class="page-title">FAQs</h1></div></div></header><div class="content-section content-section--content"><div class="textblock"><h1><span id="masters-in-applied-data-science-faqs" data-label="Master’s in Applied Data Science FAQs" class="anchor-point"></span>Master’s in Applied Data Science FAQs</h1><p style="text-align: left;"><span style="color: #800000;"><em>Learn more about what makes our program unique.</em></span></p><p><iframe title="YouTube video player" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/XS_eAV7NfrA?si=YAwLa5C3CAWLbyOZ" width="560" height="315" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen="allowfullscreen"></iframe><br /> David Uminsky, PhD – UChicago Data Science Institute, Executive Director</p></div></div><div class="list-accordion"><ul class="accordion" data-responsive-accordion-tabs="accordion medium-tabs"><li class="accordion-item is-active" data-accordion-item> <a href="#" class="accordion-title">Application Process</a><div class="accordion-content" data-tab-content><ul class="accordion" data-accordion data-multi-expand="true" data-allow-all-closed="true"><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-0"
class="accordion-title"> When will I receive my Master's in Applied Data Science admission decision? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-0"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Admissions decisions are typically released 1-2 months after each application deadline. Only completed applications are reviewed. Please refer to the <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/ms-in-applied-data-science/how-to-apply/">How to Apply</a> page for guidelines.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-1"
class="accordion-title"> If I finish my Master's in Applied Data Science application before the deadline, will I receive my decision early? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-1"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>No, admissions decisions for the in-person program are typically released 1-2 months after each application deadline. Your application must be complete to be considered for review.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-2"
class="accordion-title"> How do I submit the materials that will accompany my Master's in Applied Data Science application? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-2"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Please review the <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/ms-in-applied-data-science/how-to-apply/">How to Apply</a> page.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-3"
class="accordion-title"> Does the admissions office allow recommenders to email their letter directly as an attachment to be included in an applicant’s file? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-3"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Unfortunately, no. Recommenders must upload their letter of support by using the URL that is sent to them electronically by our online application system.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-4"
class="accordion-title"> Do I need to provide my recommenders with instructions? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-4"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>No. Recommendation forms and instructions are sent electronically to recommenders once their names are entered within the online application.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-5"
class="accordion-title"> My recommender did not receive notification, can I resend it? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-5"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Yes. If a recommender does not receive a URL, the applicant can resend the link through the online application or ask the recommender to check their spam folder.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-6"
class="accordion-title"> What materials do I need to submit to accompany my application for admission to the Masters in Applied Data Science program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-6"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Please review the <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/ms-in-applied-data-science/how-to-apply/">How to Apply</a> page.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-7"
class="accordion-title"> Once I upload my unofficial transcripts to my application, do I still need to provide an official transcript? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-7"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>You must upload one unofficial transcript from each university you attended within your application. An unofficial undergraduate transcript is required, even if you hold advanced degrees. Do not mail transcripts with your application; only uploads are needed for evaluation. If admitted, you will need to submit official transcripts from each university before matriculation.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-8"
class="accordion-title"> Is the GRE or GMAT required for the Master's in Applied Data Science program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-8"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>No, the GRE/GMAT is not required for admissions.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-9"
class="accordion-title"> I took the GRE and/or GMAT and want to include my score(s) with my Master's in Applied Data Science application. </a><div
id="accordion-tab-9"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>While the GRE/GMAT is not required, applicants can still submit their scores. The GRE school code is 1832; the GMAT school code is H9X-WG-70.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-10"
class="accordion-title"> Who is exempt from providing proof of English proficiency? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-10"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Please refer to the University of Chicago’s <a href="https://grad.uchicago.edu/admissions/apply/english-language-requirements/">English Language Proficiency</a> requirements.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-11"
class="accordion-title"> How will I be notified that I am admitted to the Master's in Applied Data Science program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-11"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Applicants will be notified to check their application portal via the email they used to submit their application.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-12"
class="accordion-title"> If I am admitted to the Master's in Applied Data Science program, what do I do next? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-12"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Have official e-transcripts sent to applieddatascience-admissions@uchicago.edu.</p><p>If your institution cannot send your documents electronically, please have them send your transcripts to the following mailing address:</p><div class="elementToProof"><i>The</i><i> </i><i>University of Chicago</i><br /> <i>Attention: MS in Applied Data Science Admissions</i></div><div class="elementToProof"><i>455 N Cityfront Plaza Dr., Suite 2800</i></div><div class="elementToProof"><i>Chicago, Illinois 60611</i></div></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-13"
class="accordion-title"> What test scores does UChicago accept as proof of English proficiency? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-13"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Please refer to the <a href="https://grad.uchicago.edu/admissions/apply/english-language-requirements/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Proof of English Proficiency</a> guidelines.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-14"
class="accordion-title"> What are the minimum scores required? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-14"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Please refer to the <a href="https://grad.uchicago.edu/admissions/apply/english-language-requirements/required-scores/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Required Minimum Score</a> guidelines.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-15"
class="accordion-title"> Where do I send my test scores? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-15"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Please send  TOEFL scores to the University of Chicago using <a href="https://grad.uchicago.edu/admissions/apply/english-language-requirements/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">these instructions</a> at the bottom of the page.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-16"
class="accordion-title"> I took the TOEFL over two years ago. Can I still use those TOEFL results? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-16"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Please refer to the <a href="https://grad.uchicago.edu/admissions/apply/english-language-requirements/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Validity guidelines</a>.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-17"
class="accordion-title"> What’s the difference between the MS in Applied Data Science and the MS in Data Science programs at UChicago? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-17"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>The two programs share a strong foundation in data science but differ in structure, location, and focus.</p><ul><li>The MS in Data Science is a 10-course program based on UChicago’s Hyde Park campus. It includes a comprehensive research project and is designed for students who want to dive deeper into the theoretical and research side of data science—often as preparation for PhD study or research-focused careers.</li><li>The MS in Applied Data Science program offers both a 12-course track and an 18-course thesis track. It is offered in online and in-person formats, with in-person classes held at the NBC Tower in downtown Chicago. The program focuses on applying data science and machine learning methods to real-world problems through hands-on coursework, industry collaborations, and a two-quarter capstone or research project.</li></ul><p>For a more detailed breakdown of the differences between the two programs, check out this article <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/news/should-you-choose-ms-in-data-science-or-ms-in-applied-data-science-at-uchicago/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">here</a>.</p></div></div></li></ul></div></li><li class="accordion-item " data-accordion-item> <a href="#" class="accordion-title">International Students</a><div class="accordion-content" data-tab-content><ul class="accordion" data-accordion data-multi-expand="true" data-allow-all-closed="true"><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-0"
class="accordion-title"> Which Master's in Applied Data Science provides students with visas? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-0"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>The full-time, <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/in-person-program/">In-Person</a> 1-Year 12-Course Program & 2-Year Thesis Track 18-Course Program are visa eligible.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-1"
class="accordion-title"> What is the total cost of tuition for the Master's in Applied Data Science program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-1"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Please refer to the <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/tuition-fees-aid/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Tuition, Fees, and Aid</a> webpage.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-2"
class="accordion-title"> Is the Master's in Applied Data Science an approved OPT/STEM program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-2"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Yes, the full-time, In-Person Master’s in Applied Data Science program is listed as a STEM-designated degree by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security for the purposes of the STEM OPT extension, allowing eligible students to apply. However, approval of STEM OPT is at the discretion of U.S. Citizenship & Immigration Services.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-3"
class="accordion-title"> Does the Master's in Applied Data Science program offer Curricular Practical Training (CPT)? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-3"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Please refer to the <a href="https://internationalaffairs.uchicago.edu/students/current-students/curricular-practical-training" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Curriculum Practical Training</a> webpage.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-4"
class="accordion-title"> I have worked in the U.S. for more than two years. Does that mean that I am exempt from the TOEFL/IELTS requirement? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-4"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Please refer to the<a href="https://grad.uchicago.edu/admissions/apply/english-language-%20requirements/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"> English Language Proficiency</a> guidelines.</p><p> </p></div></div></li></ul></div></li><li class="accordion-item " data-accordion-item> <a href="#" class="accordion-title">Online Program</a><div class="accordion-content" data-tab-content><ul class="accordion" data-accordion data-multi-expand="true" data-allow-all-closed="true"><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-0"
class="accordion-title"> If I am a student in the In-Person Master's in Applied Data Science Program, may I take courses in the Online Program? Conversely, if I am a student in the Online Program, may I take courses in the In-Person program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-0"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Currently, students may only take Master’s in Applied Data Science courses in the modality in which they are officially enrolled.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-1"
class="accordion-title"> Do I need to be a US citizen or permanent resident to apply to Master's in Applied Data Science Online Program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-1"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>No, students do not have to be US citizen or resident to partake in the <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/online-program/">Online Program</a>. Please note that the <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/online-program/">Online Program</a> is <em>not</em> eligible for visa sponsorship.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-2"
class="accordion-title"> How will enrolling in Master's in Applied Data Science Online Program impact my schedule? Are classes held synchronously, asynchronously, or both? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-2"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Classes generally take place on evenings and weekends in order to allow our students and instructors to maintain their professional schedules. The Master’s in Applied Data Science <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/online-program/">Online Program</a> is both synchronous and asynchronous. The same as our <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/in-person-program/">In-Person</a> program, students are required to participate in weekly, live meetings with their instructors and peers, complete readings and coursework, and engage in discussion.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-3"
class="accordion-title"> Will enrolling in Master's in Applied Data Science Online Program give me the opportunity to network with on-campus students, faculty/instructors, and advisors? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-3"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Yes. All Master’s in Applied Data Science Online Program students are invited to an annual ‘<a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/online-program/">Immersion Weekend</a>’ where attendees have opportunities to network and participate in other activities. On a rolling basis, our Career Services team will advertise additional opportunities to connect with employers and peers (e.g., virtual career fairs, virtual career advising/coaching appointments, and more).</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-4"
class="accordion-title"> What value do employers place on the Master's in Applied Data Science Online degree? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-4"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>The value employers place on the Master’s in Applied Data Science degree is significant. As they hire Data Scientist, Data Engineers, and Data Analysts from the University of Chicago the expectations for technical competence, communication and influence skills, and exposure to advanced Data Science evolving technologies is high. The skills learned in the program translate directly into practice due to the program’s balance between theory and rigorous application experience developed in coursework and the Capstone project work delivered across the curriculum.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-5"
class="accordion-title"> Is the Master's in Applied Data Science Online program equally academically rigorous as the In-Person program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-5"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Yes. The <a href="https://datascience.uchicago.edu/education/masters-programs/online-program/">Online Program</a> curriculum is overseen by the same faculty curriculum committee as the In-Person program. Both programs are jointly reviewed and are held to the same high standards. Additionally, both programs are granted by the University of Chicago <a href="https://physicalsciences.uchicago.edu/">Physical Sciences Division</a>.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-6"
class="accordion-title"> Will my diploma indicate I completed the Master's in Applied Data Science Program Online? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-6"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>No, your diploma will <em>not</em> include ‘Online’ in the name of your degree.</p></div></div></li></ul></div></li><li class="accordion-item " data-accordion-item> <a href="#" class="accordion-title">MBA/MS</a><div class="accordion-content" data-tab-content><ul class="accordion" data-accordion data-multi-expand="true" data-allow-all-closed="true"><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-0"
class="accordion-title"> How do I apply to the MBA/MS joint degree program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-0"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Applicants interested in the Joint MBA/MS degree will apply through <a href="https://www.chicagobooth.edu/admissions/apply">Booth’s centralized, joint-application process</a>. Applicants should complete the Chicago Booth Full-Time MBA <a href="https://www.chicagobooth.edu/admissions/apply">application</a> and select the MBA/MS in Applied Data Science as their program of interest. An MBA/MS program supplement will be available for completion within your Booth application. The supplement contains Applied Data Science specific questions that will be reviewed by the Applied Data Science admissions team along with your full Booth application. For complete consideration, applicants should complete the MBA application and the joint degree program supplement in the same application round prior to submitting the application.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-1"
class="accordion-title"> What courses will I take in the MBA/MS program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-1"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>As a student in the <a href="https://www.chicagobooth.edu/mba/joint-degree/mba-ms-applied-data-science">joint-degree MBA and Applied Data Science program</a>, you’ll take the equivalent of 23 100-unit courses:</p><ul><li>14 MBA classes</li><li>9 data science courses</li><li>Leadership Effectiveness and Development (LEAD)</li><li>Qualified Work Experience, a noncredit professional internship experience</li></ul><p>Your Booth courses will be in person, while your MS courses will be online. Most students will earn both degrees in seven quarters—the same time it takes to earn the MBA.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-2"
class="accordion-title"> Will MBA/MS courses be in-person or online? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-2"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Your Booth courses will be in person, while your MS courses will be online. Most students will earn both degrees in seven quarters—the same time it takes to earn the MBA. A combination of online and in-person courses gives you flexibility in course scheduling, and you’ll earn two degrees in the time it would take to complete the MBA alone.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-3"
class="accordion-title"> Are standardized tests required for admission? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-3"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>As part of the online application, candidates will be required to submit a GMAT or GRE score for the joint program. International applicants may be required to submit proof of English language proficiency by submitting a TOEFL iBT or IELTS test score. The minimum TOEFL iBT score required for admission is 104; the minimum IELTS score required is 7. Proof of English proficiency may be waived under certain criteria noted by UChicago GRAD Admissions.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-4"
class="accordion-title"> What are the main differences in programs and outcomes between the MBA/MS in Applied Data Science compared to Computer Science? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-4"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>The fields of Statistics, Mathematics, and Computer Science intersect with industry domains in different ways. The <a href="https://www.chicagobooth.edu/mba/joint-degree/mpcs-mba">MPCS</a> program focuses on the center of Computer Science, including Software Engineering, High Performance Computing, Data Analytics, and Application Development. The MS-ADS Program focuses at the intersection of multiple fields, such as Computer Science, Mathematics, and Statistics (including Statistical Inference, Linear/Non-Linear Models, Machine Learning, Natural Language Processing, and Deep Learning). The outcomes for MPCS students include Software Engineer (Developer), Senior Software Engineering Management, Software/Hardware Architect, and Senior Cyber Security Engineer. The outcomes for students in MS-ADS include roles as Data Scientist (most common), Senior Data Science Consultant, Business Intelligence (BI) Director, Data Visualization Manager, Data Analytics Engineer, and AI Solution Architect.</p></div></div></li></ul></div></li><li class="accordion-item " data-accordion-item> <a href="#" class="accordion-title">2-Year Thesis Track Program</a><div class="accordion-content" data-tab-content><ul class="accordion" data-accordion data-multi-expand="true" data-allow-all-closed="true"><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-0"
class="accordion-title"> What is the new 2-year thesis track program (18 courses), and how is it different from the 1-year program (12 courses)? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-0"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Beginning in academic 2026-27, a limited number of In-Person, Full-Time students will have the opportunity to complete a 2-year version of the MS in Applied Data Science program. The 2-year program is completed over 21 months (2 academic years). Students in the 2-year program will complete 18 instead of 12 courses. The additional 6 courses consist of 4 additional elective courses (100 units each); and 2 required thesis courses (100 units each) that culminate in the completion of a required written thesis or thesis project. The thesis will be an extension of students’ previously completed Capstone Project. 2-year program students are highly encouraged to complete a research Capstone (as compared to a traditional, industry Capstone) for various reasons.</p><p>The longer degree timeline offers more time to engage in academic and professional development while maintaining the same rigorous core curriculum and access to UChicago’s faculty and data science network. The first 12 of 18 courses that students complete in the 2-year program follow exactly the same course progression as those in the 1-year, 12-course program.</p><p>The 2-year program is not currently available to those in the Online program and/or those pursuing the MS in Applied Data Science on a part-time basis.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-1"
class="accordion-title"> When can I apply for the 2-year thesis track program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-1"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>The application for the Autumn 2026 2-Year Thesis Track closed on December 4, 2025. Future application timelines will be announced once available. Those who are not admitted to the 2-year program will be automatically considered for the 1-year 12 course program unless they choose to opt-out of consideration.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-2"
class="accordion-title"> May I switch between the 1- and 2-year programs after officially enrolling in the MS-ADS program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-2"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>No. Once admitted to the 1-year program, there is no opportunity to enroll in the 2-year program. Those admitted to the 2-year program might, under extenuating case by case circumstances, graduate early after completing the initial 12 courses of the MS degree (4-5 quarters). Any such student must meet with program faculty and staff by the required deadlines in order to maintain good academic standing and/or visa compliance (if applicable).</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-3"
class="accordion-title"> If I am admitted to the In-Person, Full-Time 1-year program, may I take any of the 2-year program electives? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-3"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>No, due to the limited number of spots available for the new 2-year program, the electives are reserved for only those students admitted to the 2-year program specifically. Should anything change, students will be notified.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-4"
class="accordion-title"> What is the 2-year program's thesis requirement? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-4"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>Those admitted to the full-time, in-person thesis track pathway (18 courses, 6 quarters of coursework) are required to complete and submit a written thesis. The thesis will be an individually authored text based on an area of interest from students’ Research Capstone Project. The thesis will be managed through the required thesis courses (2, 100 units each). A lead faculty member with knowledge of the student’s area of interest will supervise the thesis project and provide resources and support as needed.</p><p>As an alternative to a traditional written thesis, students may opt to complete a thesis project that results in a ‘minimum viable product’ project. Through this option, students will complete a rigorous, multi-step ‘build’ and ‘marketing’ phase throughout the required 2 thesis courses. The thesis project will also be supervised by a program faculty member.</p></div></div></li><li class="accordion__item" data-accordion-item><div class="accordion__indicator"></div> <a
href="#accordion-tab-5"
class="accordion-title"> How many spots are available in the new 2-year program? </a><div
id="accordion-tab-5"
class="accordion__content"
data-tab-content ><div class="textblock"><p>While subject to change, there are a projected ~50-55 spots available for the 2-year program. For application year 2025-26 (for entrance in autumn 2026), applicants must apply by the round two application deadline (December 4, 2025) in order to be considered for the 2-year program. Students admitted to the 2-year program will take the same 12 courses as those in the 1-year program, but they will complete 6 more courses over 2 additional quarters in their 2nd year of study for a total of 18 courses.</p></div></div></li></ul></div></li></ul></div></div></div></div></section></div></main><footer class="site-footer" id="site-footer" role="contentinfo"><div
